headline: CEO and co-founder of DatologyAI, focused on automated data curation for better AI model training
title: CEO & Co-Founder
confidenceNote: Multiple primary sources confirm Ari Morcos as DatologyAI co-founder and CEO, with prior FAIR, DeepMind, and Harvard neuroscience background.
bioMarkdown: CEO and co-founder of DatologyAI, where he works on automated data curation to improve AI model training. He previously conducted AI research at FAIR and DeepMind and has a PhD in neuroscience from Harvard.

primary.label: Ari Morcos personal site
primary.note: Personal site states he is co-founder and CEO of DatologyAI and describes the company's automated data curation focus.

primary.label: DatologyAI about page
primary.note: Company page lists Ari Morcos as Co-Founder and CEO and summarizes his FAIR, DeepMind, NeurIPS, ICLR, and Harvard background.

primary.label: Ari Morcos X profile
primary.note: Social profile identifies him as CEO and co-founder of DatologyAI, formerly at Meta AI FAIR and DeepMind.

secondary.label: Imbue podcast profile
secondary.note: Interview page describes DatologyAI's focus on training-data intervention and Morcos's research background.

secondary.label: Google Scholar profile
secondary.note: Scholar profile supports his AI, deep learning, and machine learning research footprint.

headline: Software engineer at Bloomberg with prior Meta and Oracle experience and an interest in enterprise AI adoption
title: Software Engineer
confidenceNote: LinkedIn search snippets and public event pages confirm Bloomberg software engineering role; limited non-LinkedIn primary detail was available.
bioMarkdown: Software engineer at Bloomberg with prior experience at Meta and Oracle. His public posts indicate interest in enterprise AI transformation and bringing AI conference learnings back into large-organization engineering contexts.

primary.label: LinkedIn profile
primary.note: Profile snippet identifies him as SWE at Bloomberg, previously at Meta and Oracle, with Purdue computer science background.

primary.label: LinkedIn AI conference post
primary.note: Post describes attending an AI conference on behalf of Bloomberg and interest in enterprise AI transformation.

secondary.label: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 schedule
secondary.note: Event schedule search result lists Arjun Harbhajanka associated with Bloomberg.

secondary.label: LinkedIn directory result
secondary.note: Directory snippet corroborates Bloomberg role and New York location.

headline: AI product and technology professional at AmbyTech
title: AI Product Lead
confidenceNote: The attendee-provided title says AI Product Lead; public LinkedIn snippets identify him as Product Manager at AmbyTech Inc. and AmbyTech's company page confirms him as an employee. Evidence is consistent but sparse.
bioMarkdown: Works on product at AmbyTech, with public profile signals around technology, entrepreneurship, design innovation, and AI-enabled customer experience tooling. Useful match for conversations on applied AI product development and early-stage product strategy.

primary.label: LinkedIn profile
primary.note: Profile snippet identifies Arjun Rao as Product Manager at AmbyTech Inc. with technology, entrepreneurship, design, and innovation positioning.

primary.label: Amby Tech LinkedIn company page
primary.note: Company page lists Arjun Rao among employees and shows a San Diego primary office address.

secondary.label: LinkedIn post snippet
secondary.note: Public post snippet ties the same LinkedIn profile to entrepreneurial interests.

secondary.label: LinkedIn post snippet
secondary.note: Public post snippet corroborates the same AmbyTech profile and UC San Diego connection.

headline: CEO and co-founder of Superconductor; previously co-founded Gradescope
title: CEO
confidenceNote: Primary and high-signal secondary sources consistently identify him as Superconductor CEO/co-founder and former Gradescope CEO/co-founder.
bioMarkdown: CEO and co-founder of Superconductor, building tools for controlling AI coding agents across browser, phone, GitHub, and Slack. He previously co-founded Gradescope, an AI-assisted grading platform acquired by Turnitin.

primary.label: LinkedIn profile
primary.note: Profile snippet lists him as CEO and Cofounder of Superconductor and former CEO and Cofounder of Gradescope.

primary.label: RubyEvents profile
primary.note: Speaker profile describes Superconductor's AI coding-agent control product and his Gradescope background.

secondary.label: Reach Capital profile
secondary.note: Profile says he is CEO and Co-founder of Superconductor, co-founder and former CEO of Gradescope, and later Chief Strategy Officer at Turnitin.

secondary.label: EdSurge author profile
secondary.note: Profile supports his Gradescope CEO/co-founder background and AI teaching roots at UC Berkeley.

headline: Co-founder and co-CEO of STOKR, building regulated tokenized capital-markets infrastructure from Luxembourg
title: Co-Founder
confidenceNote: STOKR press releases and public profiles consistently identify Arnab Naskar as co-founder, co-CEO or business lead of STOKR, focused on regulated tokenized assets.
bioMarkdown: Co-founder of STOKR, a Luxembourg-based platform for regulated tokenized securities and alternative assets. His work centers on Bitcoin-based capital-markets infrastructure, digital securities, and compliant access to tokenized investment products.

primary.label: LinkedIn profile
primary.note: Profile snippet identifies him as co-founder and co-CEO of STOKR, focused on tokenizing financial assets.

primary.label: STOKR tokenized asset volume announcement
primary.note: STOKR announcement quotes Arnab Naskar as Co-Founder and Co-CEO and describes the platform's Bitcoin-based capital-markets vision.

primary.label: STOKR funding announcement
primary.note: Company press release identifies him as STOKR Co-CEO and discusses STOKR's asset-tokenization strategy.

secondary.label: Around The Coin podcast profile
secondary.note: Podcast profile says Arnab co-founded STOKR and oversees business strategy for open and decentralized capital markets.

secondary.label: BankingHub interview article
secondary.note: Article states Tobias Seidl and Arnab Naskar founded STOKR in 2018 as an investment marketplace for alternative assets.

secondary.label: F6S STOKR profile
secondary.note: Startup profile places Arnab Naskar in Luxembourg and notes his LegalTech and FinTech interests.
